caption:
~
Thank you to everyone who came and surfed the net with us at Unprofessional Variety Show: The Internet. A highlight was when audience members who were adults before our digital overlord shared things they miss: breakups while standing at a pay phone on the street. Ugh, so good. 

NEXT SHOW: May 10

PERFORMERS

📱Mike Albo @albomike
💻Li-Ming Hu @outraged994
🩻Libby Paloma @libbys_arty_party
🤖Mac Waters
💿Michael aka Micro our cyber securities expert 

•Emceed by Maya Suess @MayaSuess

The Parkside Lounge @the_parkside_lounge_nyc 

~
• Go buy Mike Albo’s amazingly hilarious book Hologram Boyfriends. 
• Li Ming as Steve Jobs
• Mac Waters bringing all the analog and digital wonders 
• Libby Paloma making tele health visits sexy
• Micro taking precautions by covering his eyes (hmm)
• Emcee Maya being an influencer 
• Emcee Maya singer her new AI assisted banger
• The amazing Vandy @vanburgocollective handling tech like a boss
• Libby, Steve Jobs and Maya back stage (also FUCK ICE)
• Steve and his money tree

Thank you @disiterate @mev_merized @elizabazile @heather_renee_russ @angelomadsen and others for videos, and to @martinthesupernaut and @tata.loperasanchez for bringing your professional documentation skills. @macauleydevun for holding down the door, @pigpenhaulin for bedazzling Libby and passing mics, and the @the_parkside_lounge_nyc staff for everything.

AND last but not least, the amazing Maegan from @evvintagecollective for dressing Maya in Y2K and New Wave looks ⚡️⚡️⚡️

~
Thank you to everyone who braved the ice to celebrate the season of the dark with us.

UNPROFESSIONAL Variety Show:
Vampires Resurrected 🩸🦇🌕⚰️

Throwback to our first Vampire show in 2022. Our undead emcee was just finding herself. Now she’s spent some time in the shadows, and ready to welcome the newly risen. Out of the coffin and onto the stage. 

SUNDAY, December 14, 7:30 PM
(Doors at 7 pm. Come early to get a good seat)

Emcee camp Maya, wearing a classic cape and low cut black dress is feeling herself in an undead trance.

Audience documentation by @davidwbullen

PERFORMER PROFILE
Our next performer, Kris Grey, is a gender queer creature of transmasculine experience. Tonight they will perform as Bat Boy, looking for love in all the dark places. Feast your eyes on the glow up of the millennia. Once a pariah, misrepresented by the tabloid press during his formative years. Decades of study has led scientists to diagnose his condition as falling under the trans umbrella; trans-species that is. Doing his best to fit into society’s ridged human-centered beauty ideals after being mocked in the satire media of the 1990s, Bat Boy spent the past three decades seeking human-affirming medical care, all of which he has paid for out of pocket due to an exclusionary clause in the healthcare system barring insurance coverage for halfbreeds. Now he is palatable for the masses, single, and ready to mingle! Please welcome to the stage Bat Boy!

A close-up of his face, Bat Boy looks us straight in the eye, he has a warm and captivating gaze, his long ears extending above his bald head. We could perhaps, find the dark with him. Drawn in pink, yellow and purple hues agains a bright purple ground.

~
PERFORMER PROFILE
Jack Rabbit Slims is the New Jersey raised and Brooklyn based Afro Latina babe wreaking havoc on the city for the last 7 years one slow burn at a time! She is a triple water sign so around her you are always in the splash zone.  Never forgetting to embrace the most important part of being sexy, the silly! Jack is known as the cult classic of burlesque.

Jack Rabbit Slims shows us why we’re all her cult followers. Open mouthed you can see a glint if her pointy incisor, she’s teasing us with one hand lifted the other cupping her curves. Her hair is coifed to perfection, a billowing crown, and she wears a green tunic open at the side and plunged low to let her cleavage shine. Drawn in colored pencil against a vibrant light blue ground by Maya.

Thank you to everyone who comes to Unprofessional Variety Show. The last show gave us some baaad baaad (and some super good) advice. But just remember, you’re fiiiiiiiiiine. 

NEXT SHOW: December 14

~
* Some of the fab performers on stage at the end of the event
* Bob Voyage giving sage travel advice
* Sebastian Adé wooing us with his sweet sounds
* Cherrie looking glam as she advises us about where to sit in the Parkside back room and what she thinks about Oprah’s role in the self help industrial (capitalism) complex
* Shannon Sennott, giving truly, excellent sex and relationship answers to anonymous questions from the audience. 
* Emcee Maya awishin’ and ahopin’ to reach her full potential, holding a stack of (terrible) self help books and wearing an @alittlebitculty T-shirt
* West Vagina dressed as the silver sliver moon speaking to our core selves
* Li-Ming talking about all the bad advice she’s gotten in her life
* Shawn Escarciga giving us, ehem, great advice
* Maya dressed in her 90’s power suit best as styled by Erin Connolly
* Maya on the stage with Sebastian, dressed in a hot striped look of oversized shorts and a hat, in the fashion of HMac. Libby Paloma’s gorgeous banner across the back, saying “BADVICE.”

~
And thank you to our camp counselors Libby, Macauley, Mamie and Vandy, for keeping all the campers in line. And to our campers who jumped up on stage to play some summer camp games.

ARTIST PROFILE
Randy @r0seandth0rn did Maya’s make up for A Magic Show, and our Magic would not have happened without theirs. 

Randy is a self-taught artist who focuses on beauty, runway and editorial makeup. Randy’s approach to artistry intentionally upholds and respects all skin tones, types and textures. With a deep understanding of essential makeup application, Randy seeks to subvert and metamorphose beauty through exploration and collaboration.

Book them for all your wild and wonderful looks, @r0seandth0rn .

~
• A person bearing their gold teeth and giving us an eye, their hair shorn close. On their gold flecked cheeks is the word “galore” written in a font like it was a metal band. 
• Purple cloak and headpiece from @versbk.nyc, the model has triangle pink lashes accentuating their eyes, silver stars and tears dot their face, their gold and purple ombre lips slightly parted. 
• Two models swathed in orange and green light, one rests their chin on the other. They are attached by long eyelashes, the bottom lids of one connecting to the top lids of the other. 
• Emcee Maya wearing the hood of the wizard garment by @tillium.nu, her curly hair framing her face. Jewels surround her shimmering eyes, and a line of iridescent silver crosses her cheeks and slides down her face, shimmering in the stage lights. 
• Emcee Maya again wearing the cape by @thewebhasnoweaver and the curricular head pics by @chrisofhur, face reflecting light.

~

A detail of The Magician card from @jess_dobkin’s Major Arcana deck from her Wetrospective, a rabbit coming out of a hat with the shadow of a certain Messiah behind it; a sigil carved into a bay leaf; pencil drawing of said sigil for protection and fortification; emcee Maya hosting the  book launch of Jess Dobkin’s Wetrospective and gearing up for tonight’s show, behind her on a screen says the word “Magic.”

~
ARTIST PROFILE
The beloved Libby Paloma, @libbys_arty_party, aka Nacho, aka Doctor Dislodge, took the last show to a whole new level not only with her too-close-for-comfort remote doctor’s appointment act, but with the creation of our emcee’s spectacular organ suit and the absurd cure-all-ailments needle. Thank you Libby!!

Libby Paloma is a queercrip, Mexican-American interdisciplinary artist working primarily in sculpture and performance. Paloma utilizes installation, labor-intensive sculpture, and performance to create delightfully dazzling pieces. Paloma’s work is a playful mode of intervening in dominant and oppressive structures to offer alternative, softer, more joyous ways of engaging with self and others.

Save the date for the next show: December 15

~
> Libby Paloma holding the giant soft needle 
> Sewing soft pink intestines 
> the organs in all their glory: intestines and some squishy red and pink organs that do important things. There are green tassels hanging from the intestines that mean something is not going so well. 
> Soft needle pieces on a blue cutting mat, a line drawing isn’t the design sits near by. 
> Emcee Maya wears the organs while she asks if she’ll still be loved tomorrow.

-Eak the Geek holding up his bed of nails in front of his tattooed torso and face.
-Xaddy Addy with two red balloons staples to their tits holding an industrial stapler in one hand. -Dr Ksenia, wearing a red clown nose, throws confetti into the air to celebrate her clown hero the incredible Oleg Popov. 
-With white clown makeup Klovis croons and screams at the piano as we travel with him through faggotry. 
-Clew comes up behind Maya being the clown she sings about in “Send in the Clowns”. They all stand together at the end of the show, and the clown-nose-cherry on top is our wonderful sound tech Kipp donning an appropriate red nose.

THANK YOU FOR COMING to Unprofessional Variety Show #9: . . . what did you expect? It was A Clown Show! 🤡

At East Village Vintage Collective (EVVC), we consider ourselves a curated thrift store. We have picked through the jumbled second hand store racks to bring you a curated collection at the same low prices. We feature tons of vintage fun! Clothes for adults and children, vinyl, tchotchkes, home goods, toys and more. By supporting us you are also supporting eco-friendly fashion, women-owned business and an EV/LES based small business! COSTUMER PROFILE
Thank you to the incredible @SarahDahlinger for making this GORGEOUS PUPPET costume, for the badass Lady Elaine Fairechilde—Thorn in the side of King Friday, the tyrannical ruler of the Neighborhood of Make Believe. A tireless fighter, standing up for all of us rule-breakers.

Sarah Dahlinger is a multimedia artist based in Queens, New York. Her highly collaborative practice involves installation, printmaking, video, sculpture, food, and costume and prop design. She co-directs @SpringboardCollective, a collaboration that creates immersive, interactive installations and social sculptures. Sarah is also a collaborating artist with Whoop Dee Doo, an artist-led project that utilizes art and performance to connect communities. She is currently the Project Coordinator for Flux Factory on Governors Island and works as a printmaking technician at the Cooper Union. Sarah is also a long-time attendee of Unprofessional Variety Show!

~
Firstly, Lady Elaine Fairchilde on stage in all her glory, proceeded by a series of in-process shots of Sarah using latex and mold making to bring LEF to life. The final slide is LEF's page from the Mr. Rogers' website. Text reads: Lady Elaine Fairchilde, She's mischievous, but she's also brave, sassy and ready to speak up. LEF wears a red shirt-dress with large round blue and white buttons, her nose and cheeks are dark red and her hair is an unbothered shock of straw.

Thank you everyone who attended UNPROFESSIONAL VARIETY SHOW #6: Won't You Be My Neighbor?

All the fun we had together, neighbors! We learned about lesbians w/ Moses Harper and her cast Nancy Crespo and Akira Bryant; We met some neighbors with Shawn Escarciga @missladysalad, like Marina Abramovic, after a long day performing the Artist is Present when she had a lot on her mind; We also learned how to take care of the neighborhood critters with Siobhan Meow; and our wishes came true with heartthrobs @earls2gearls!! We learned our numbers and colors with Emcee Rogers, with the help of the book, The Wall of Vagina by Parkside Neighbor, @kembrapfahler_, oh, and we can't forget our special visit by premier puppet, joining us from the Neighborhood of Make Believe, Lady Elaine Fairchilde (amazing costume by @sarahdahlinger).

Keep your eyes out for the next Unprofessional in September!!!

PERFORMER PROFILE
We were so so sad that Shawn Escarciga @missladysalad, couldn't make the last show, but THEY'RE BACK on MAY 14!!!! 

Shawn is an artist who does things IRL and URL. Shawn's work has explored labor and class; questioning value systems and hierarchies; and ways to balance being silly with tangible moves towards equity and care. Their work encompasses performance, comedy, memes, language, and organizing, and has been shown across NYC, nationally and internationally at galleries, basements, museums, sex parties, on little and bigger screens, and in the hearts of every f-slur who dared to dream. More at @missladysalad and shawnescarciga.com

COSTUMER PROFILE
Tits for days, a secret Johnny in the back, and a vag with something to say. Thank you Jesse Harrod @jesseharrod1 for making this INCREDIBLE birthday suit. 

Lucky for us, emcee @mayasuess has friends with skills and a great sense of humor. 

Jesse Harrod is an artist whose practice explores embodiment, gender, and sexual identity. Working with multiple media forms and materials, Harrod’s work builds on herstories of 1970s feminist art to offer queer imaginations of the body, from the abject and the grotesque to the humorous.

Jesse Harrod has an MFA from the department of Fiber & Material Studies from the School of The Art Institute of Chicago and a BFA from the Nova Scotia College of Art and Design University. She is currently the Head of Fibers & Material Studies at Tyler School of Art in Philadelphia. Check out more of their work at jesseharrod.com

Next Unprofessional: May 14
@the_parkside_lounge_nyc
Put it in your calendar nowwww!!!!

~
Emcee Maya wears this stunning birthday suit to do a strip-tease-reveal. It has two sets of tits, the top ones with tassels for nipples, a yawning vagina, and penis, balls and man nipples on the back. The following three images are some of Jesse's beautiful work, SUPport System, Heavy Metal and and a large scale brightly colored hanging macrame installation, in the show Mending and Repair in Response at the Fleisher Ollman gallery in 2019.

Video by @sarahdahlinger

~
COSTUMER PROFILE
Sneak peek into the making of the outfit that the ever-fabulous Dew @suppressingyays created for @MayaSuess at the last Unprofessional, Birthday Suit edition. 

This birthday suit was soooo 🔥🔥🔥 with its pixelated titty stars and tiny external undies.

Dew is a poet, screenwriter, fashion designer, costumer, first generation American, and all round curious animal of the world. Dew is also Unprofessional's tech assist, playing songs and visuals from the front of the room. We couldn't do it without Dew.

Stay tuned for the next, Unprofessional, May 14 @the_parkside_lounge_nyc

~
Maya back stage showing off the look, which is a cute shorts and well shoulder padded bollero jacket, with excellent lapels. Made in a heavy cotton in a light cream tone. The breast of the jacket has star-burst shapes made out of hand-painted pixelated squares that resemble digitally blurred out nipples. tacked on to the shorts, there is a tiny cut out in the shape of underwear, made from the same pixelated textile.
Next, Dew and Maya grin in Dew's studio, Maya wearing the muslin pattern version of the suit in process. Then the emcee doing her thing in The Look, and finally once more, this time w/ the ever wonderful Nacho (@libby_arty_party).

ONE DAY AWAY . . . 
SUNDAY, February 26, 7:30 pm
Unprofessional Variety Show #5: 
Birthday Suit Edition!
Sliding scale $10 - $25
All funds go to the performers

We've had a couple of performer swaps this time, the brilliant Ciclón @bestial_provocations, who brought us the unforgettable stand-up lecture performance last year, is celebrating their partner #chetocastellano #thelegendofcastellano, and will be back with us another time.

And sadly, the unforgettable Shawn Escarciga @missladysalad, is wrestling with a mean virus! (bodies, sheesh). But never fear, trusty audience, Shawn will be back to wow us with impressions and mayhem soon enough!

We are THRILLED to have the incredible Fareeha Khan @fareeeezy performing, and (INTRODUCING) the never dull wily wilds of Libby paloma @libby_art_party (Performer Profile to come), who will bring the party to this birthday party.

Some weird bulbous doodle with the words "Birthday suit" hidden in between lumps, followed by a drawing of each of the performers (Libby coming soon), a back stage image of @mayasuess' drawing table, with colored pencils all around a drawing of Mistah Coles, and finally a detail of the butt poster image.

We’re getting on more 2022 lists!! Pretty good for our first year on the small stage ☺️ 

Thank you to @angelomadsen for naming us in @thequeerreview’s LGBTQ+ highlights of 2002, and yes to the work of @_anna_campbell_!!

Shout outs to performers @bestial_provocations and @jaceogram & @lpvgarden, and our emcee @mayasuess 

~
The front of the 2022 review with a grid of amazing queer art and culture, followed by images of the text. About Unprofessional Madsen says:

“Unprofessional Variety Show is a new Lower East Side gem of 2022. The show runs quarterly at the Parkside Lounge. I can now say that I’ve witnessed a performative lecture in homage to the artist’s dog’s beautiful vulva (Ciclón), I’ve time traveled to Weimar Berlin’s lesbian bars with host-performer Maya Suess, I’ve been indoctrinated into bizarre mythic birthing rituals (Peter Cramer and Jack Waters), and have been persistently reminded that there is humor and joy still in the world.”

Thank you again Megan Milks, @sklimnagem, for writing about Unprofessional as a literature inspiration for 2022 for @bombmag. 

We’ll do our best to keep the swervy antics up in 2023 and beyond. 

See you on February 26!! 

~
Text from Bomb Magazine written by Megan Milks describing a couple of creative inspirations found outside of literature in 2022. The fifth slide is a drawing of Megan done for the second iteration of Unprofessional, with them looking dapper against a pink ground.

3rd & B’zaar is a pop-up market and event space located at 191 East 3rd St (Storefront West), between Ave A and Ave B, in Manhattan’s East Village, NYC.

East Village Vintage Collective (EVVC) began as a brick and mortar store in Manhattan, NYC, which we opened in August 2015 with an aspiration to bring back some of the old school East Village vibe that we loved so much. At EVVC, we like to consider ourselves a curated thrift store. We have picked through the jumbled second hand store racks to bring you a curated collection at the same low prices. We feature tons of vintage fun! Clothes for adults and children, vinyl, tchotchkes, home goods, toys and more. By supporting us you are also supporting eco-friendly fashion, women-owned business and small business! Go you!!

Thank you to everyone who came to Unprofessional 2.0: Pasts and Futures. 

The performances were the best that a lavender night could offer. 
@sachayanow @fernandovieirausa @poisonaivy69 #JDPluecker

@sklimnagem brought their time machine made by pasting letters from THE TALE OF PLAGUES AND CARNIVALS by Samuel Delany onto their keyboard.

@vague_static arrived as the Great Being, the patron bitch of variety show and cabaret, the deity of all things raunchy and distasteful, the cosmic point where humor and discomfort suck face, the protector of all sacred weirdos . . . 

Thank you to @bushybussybully for playing accompaniment to the song "Das Lila-Lied," sung by our emcee @mayasuess. She didn't lose her eyelashes or comedic timing this time. 

The incredible work of the deeply missed performer @daniellejabrams was also present. 💔

Dance party tunes by @expertfundude, documentation by @bestial_provocations. Thank you Courntey Jordan for working the door, and of course, the always wonderful @gavinodownie and @the_parkside_lounge_nyc.

The next Unprofessional will be September 11. We will see you there!! 🔮

Photos by @davidwbullen @angelomadsen @sarahdahlinger & @bestial_provocations

Emcee Maya in her birthday cake dress, CICLON showing us a picture of her dog friend's holes, Jack reading from the scroll, Peter whispering from the beyond, Cherish telling us a lil story, Klovis playing out his feelings, Maya and Birthday Cake (she goes by “Cay”).

Check out this DIY flyer that CICLÓN @bestial_provocations made for their performance this weekend. We love their interspecies ways. . . 

"My performance will take companion species heartache and grief and try to make it entertaining, lusy and maybe even funny. I will be who I am – an exhibitionist. I will let you into my dirty lewd perverted wierido mind and image archive. . .

Ciclon is a necromancer and trans* media storyteller that is moved by quantum connectivities within and beyond the technosphere. Their recent work establishes meaningful connections amongst goddexxes, ghosts, animals, plants, and the elements. They seek to amplify spectra of desire."

~
a flyer made on plywood, with lots of differerent colors and fonts. The title is "Postvalentine Bestial Provocations, Performance by CICLON" There is a line drawing of a cat.

We will see you at @the_parkside_lounge_nyc on February 20 at 7:30.

There is no cover, but plan to tip the performers and the bartenders. Come and support this amazing Queer/Trans run, longtime LES establisment, that’s been rocked by the Pandemic. Look at how beautiful it is!!!

There is a ramp available and there is an accessible bathroom. Door is 32” wide to enter. Please let us know if you need any assistance with accessibility. 🎉

~
